login.title: Sign in to your workspace
login.subtitle: Enter your credentials to continue.
login.saml_not_configured: SAML SSO is not configured yet. Please sign in with email and password.
login.setup_success_banner: Admin created. Sign in to continue.
login.password_reset_success_banner: Password updated. Sign in to continue.

forgot_password.title: Reset your password
forgot_password.subtitle: Enter your email and we'll send a reset link.
forgot_password.success_body: If an account with that email exists, we have sent a reset link.
forgot_password.error_generic: Could not send a reset link. Please try again.

password_reset.title: Create a new password
password_reset.subtitle: You are resetting the password for {{email}}.
password_reset.error_invalid_token: This reset link is invalid or has expired.

error.oauth2_login_failed: We could not complete the sign-in with your provider. Please try again.
error.oauth2_email_missing: Your provider did not return an email address. Ask your administrator to grant the email scope.
error.oauth2_email_unverified: Your email address is not verified with the provider. Verify it and try again.
error.oauth2_local_email_conflict: An AccessFlow account with this email already uses a password. Ask an administrator to switch the account to single sign-on before retrying.
error.oauth2_unexpected_auth: Single sign-on returned an unexpected authentication state.
error.oauth2_unknown_provider: Single sign-on returned an unknown provider.
error.oauth2_org_not_allowed: Your provider account does not belong to an organization that AccessFlow allows.
error.oauth2_email_domain_not_allowed: Your email address is not in an AccessFlow-allowed domain.
error.account_disabled: Your AccessFlow account is currently disabled.
error.generic: Single sign-on failed. Please try again.

error.saml_login_failed: We could not complete the sign-in via SAML. Please try again.
error.saml_email_missing: Your identity provider did not return an email attribute. Ask your administrator to update the attribute mapping.
error.saml_local_email_conflict: An AccessFlow account with this email already uses a password. Ask an administrator to switch the account to single sign-on before retrying.
error.saml_signature_invalid: The SAML response signature did not match the configured IdP certificate. Ask your administrator to verify the certificate.
error.saml_assertion_invalid: The SAML response could not be validated. Please try again or ask your administrator to verify the IdP configuration.
error.saml_not_configured: SAML SSO is not configured for this deployment.
error.saml_unexpected_auth: SAML SSO returned an unexpected authentication state.
error.account_disabled: Your AccessFlow account is currently disabled.
error.generic: SAML sign-on failed. Please try again.

setup.title: Create the first admin
setup.subtitle: No active admin user was found. Set up your organization and the first admin account to get started.
setup.footer: first-run setup · only available while no admin exists

smtp.title: Configure system SMTP (optional)
smtp.subtitle: Set up email delivery so you can invite teammates. You can change this later from the notifications page.

invitation.title: Activate your account
invitation.subtitle: Set a password to complete your AccessFlow invitation.
invitation.greeting: You have been invited to {{organization}} as {{role}}.

validation.totp_code_pattern: Enter the 6-digit code from your authenticator.

oauth2.client_id_max: Client ID must be at most 512 characters.
oauth2.client_secret_max: Client secret must be at most 2048 characters.
oauth2.scopes_max: Scopes override must be at most 1024 characters.
oauth2.tenant_id_max: Tenant ID must be at most 255 characters.
oauth2.allowed_max: List must contain at most 100 entries.
oauth2.allowed_entry_max: Each entry must be at most 255 characters.
oauth2.display_name_max: Display name must be at most 255 characters.
oauth2.uri_invalid: Must be a valid http(s) URL.
oauth2.uri_max: URL must be at most 2048 characters.
oauth2.attribute_max: Claim name must be at most 255 characters.
oauth2.base_url_invalid: Server base URL must be a valid https URL.
oauth2.base_url_must_be_origin: Server base URL must be an origin (scheme + host + optional port) — no path, query, or fragment.

system_smtp.from_address_invalid: From address must be a valid email.

slack.app_id_max: App ID must be at most 64 characters
slack.default_channel_max: Default channel ID must be at most 64 characters
slack.bot_token_max: Bot token must be at most 512 characters
slack.signing_secret_max: Signing secret must be at most 255 characters

validation.attestation_description_max: Description must be at most 2000 characters
validation.attestation_datasource_required: A datasource is required for datasource-scoped campaigns
validation.attestation_due_after_open: Due time must be after the scheduled open time
validation.lifecycle_description_max: Description must be at most 2,000 characters
validation.lifecycle_target_table_size: Target table must be at most 255 characters
validation.lifecycle_classification_tag_size: Classification tag must be at most 100 characters
validation.lifecycle_timestamp_column_size: Timestamp column must be at most 255 characters
validation.lifecycle_retention_window_size: Retention window must be at most 50 characters
validation.lifecycle_soft_delete_column_size: Soft-delete column must be at most 255 characters

errors.auth_generic: Sign in failed. Please try again.
errors.setup_already_complete: Setup is already complete — please sign in.
errors.email_already_exists: A user with that email already exists.
errors.setup_generic: Could not complete setup. Please try again.
errors.server_unreachable: Could not reach the AccessFlow server. Please retry.
errors.review_self_forbidden: You cannot review a query you submitted yourself.
errors.reviewer_not_eligible: You are not an approver at this stage.
errors.review_query_not_pending: This query is no longer pending review.
errors.query_not_cancellable: This query can no longer be cancelled (it has already advanced).
errors.query_cancel_generic: Could not cancel the query. Please try again.
errors.query_snapshot_not_found: No execution snapshot exists for this query. Only executed queries can be replayed.
errors.replay_schema_incompatible: The target datasource is not compatible with this query.
errors.replay_schema_incompatible_tables: The target datasource is missing tables this query needs: {{tables}}.
errors.query_replay_generic: Could not replay the query. Please try again.
errors.review_query_not_found: This query is no longer available.
errors.review_generic: Could not record the review decision. Please try again.
errors.review_plan_in_use: This review plan is attached to a datasource and cannot be deleted.
errors.review_plan_name_already_exists: A review plan with that name already exists. Pick a different name.
errors.illegal_review_plan: That review plan configuration is not allowed.
errors.review_plan_generic: Could not save the review plan. Please try again.
errors.datasource_name_already_exists: A datasource with that name already exists in your organization. Pick a different name.
errors.ai_config_name_already_exists: An AI configuration with that name already exists. Pick a different name.
errors.ai_config_in_use: This AI configuration is bound to one or more datasources and cannot be deleted.
errors.illegal_user_operation_admin: This user change is not permitted (admins cannot demote or deactivate themselves).
errors.notification_channel_config_invalid: Channel configuration is missing required fields.
errors.notification_delivery_failed: Test notification could not be delivered.
errors.bad_audit_query: Audit log filter is invalid (page size or resource type).
errors.datasource_permission_already_exists: This user already has a permission row for this datasource.
errors.illegal_datasource_permission: That user is not part of your organization.
errors.datasource_grant_generic: Failed to grant access. Please try again.
errors.password_reset_expired: This reset link has expired. Request a new one.
errors.password_reset_already_used: This reset link has already been used.
errors.password_reset_revoked: This reset link is no longer valid. Request a new one.
errors.password_reset_generic: Could not reset your password. Please try again.

driver_unavailable.offline_cache_miss: The JDBC driver for {{dbType}} is not in the local cache and AccessFlow is in offline mode. Pre-populate the driver cache or set ACCESSFLOW_DRIVERS_OFFLINE=false and restart AccessFlow. ({{detail}})
driver_unavailable.cache_not_writable: AccessFlow cannot write to the JDBC driver cache directory. Set ACCESSFLOW_DRIVER_CACHE to a writable path (for example, ~/.accessflow/drivers) and restart AccessFlow. ({{detail}})
driver_unavailable.download_failed: AccessFlow could not download the {{dbType}} JDBC driver from Maven Central. Check network connectivity, or set ACCESSFLOW_DRIVERS_REPOSITORY_URL to your internal Maven mirror and retry. ({{detail}})
driver_unavailable.checksum_mismatch: The downloaded {{dbType}} JDBC driver failed integrity verification. Delete the cached file and retry; if the problem persists, the configured Maven mirror may be tampering with the JAR. ({{detail}})
driver_unavailable.unavailable: The {{dbType}} JDBC driver cannot be loaded. Ask your AccessFlow administrator to verify the driver cache and registry. ({{detail}})

errors.admin_generic: Could not complete the request. Please try again.
errors.language_not_in_allowed_list: That language is not in the organization's allowed languages.
errors.totp_required: Enter the code from your authenticator app to continue.
errors.totp_invalid: That verification code is not valid. Please try again.
errors.totp_invalid_code: That verification code is not valid.
errors.password_change_not_allowed: Password change is not allowed for this account.
errors.profile_generic: Could not update your profile. Please try again.
errors.custom_driver_duplicate: A JDBC driver with this SHA-256 is already registered for your organization.
errors.custom_driver_in_use_one: Cannot delete: {{count}} datasource still uses this driver. Delete that datasource first.
errors.custom_driver_in_use_other: Cannot delete: {{count}} datasources still use this driver. Delete those datasources first.
errors.custom_driver_checksum_mismatch: The uploaded JAR's SHA-256 does not match the value you entered. Recompute the checksum and try again.
errors.custom_driver_invalid_jar: The uploaded JAR does not contain {{driverClass}}, or that class does not implement java.sql.Driver.
errors.custom_driver_too_large: The JAR exceeds the {{maxMb}} MB upload limit. Strip unused classes or split the bundle.
errors.custom_driver_validation: Some required upload fields are missing or invalid.
errors.custom_driver_generic: Could not upload the JDBC driver. Please try again.
errors.routing_policy_priority_conflict: Another routing policy already uses this priority.
errors.routing_policy_generic: Could not save the routing policy. Please try again.
errors.connector_install_failed: Could not install the connector driver. Check connectivity and try again.
errors.connector_generic: Something went wrong with the connector. Please try again.
errors.quota_exceeded: This organization has reached one of its quotas.
errors.organization_generic: Could not save the organization. Please try again.
errors.collaboration_forbidden: You can't collaborate on this query.
errors.collaboration_generic: Something went wrong with the comment.
errors.dashboard_generic: Something went wrong on the dashboard

text_to_sql.label: Describe your query
text_to_sql.placeholder: e.g. order numbers for the last 5 days
text_to_sql.success: Query generated — review it before submitting
text_to_sql.error: Could not generate a query from your description
text_to_sql.error_with_reason: Could not generate a query from your description: {{reason}}

editor.title: SQL editor
editor.subtitle: Submit a query for AI and human review.
editor.justification_placeholder: Why are you running this query?
editor.dry_run_error: Could not produce a dry-run plan
editor.schedule_help: If set, the approved query runs automatically at that time.
editor.schedule_in_past_error: Scheduled time must be in the future.

review_plan_preview.empty_description: This datasource has no review plan. Submit will go straight to execution policy.

templates.empty_state: No templates yet — save the current SQL to start your library.
templates.delete_confirm_body: Delete template ‘{{name}}’? This cannot be undone.
templates.load_modal_intro_one: Fill in {{count}} placeholder to substitute before loading.
templates.load_modal_intro_other: Fill in {{count}} placeholders to substitute before loading.

history.title: Version history — {{name}}
history.restore_confirm_body: Restore version {{number}}? This creates a new version; history is preserved.
history.restore_success: Template restored to the selected version

fields.name: Name
fields.description_placeholder: Optional — describe what this query does
fields.pin_to_datasource_help: When pinned, the template only shows up when this datasource is selected.

validation.name_size: Name must be at most 128 characters
validation.description_max: Description must be at most 1000 characters
validation.tags_max: A template may have at most 10 tags
validation.tag_size: Each tag must be at most 32 characters

editor.break_glass_modal_warning_body: The query executes immediately without approval. All administrators are notified, the action is prominently audited, and it must be reviewed afterwards.

dry_run_panel.title: Execution plan
dry_run_panel.empty_prompt: Run a dry run to preview the execution plan and estimated impact.
dry_run_panel.unsupported: Dry run is not supported for this engine.
dry_run_panel.no_plan: The engine returned no plan for this query.

ai_panel.title: AI analysis
ai_panel.empty_prompt_analyze: Click Analyze to run AI review on this query.
ai_panel.stale_banner: This analysis ran against an earlier version of the query. Re-run it to refresh the results.

list.title: Query history
list.subtitle: All queries you've submitted or have access to view.
list.export_truncated: Export truncated at 50,000 rows. Apply tighter filters to capture the full result set.

detail.no_justification: Requester did not provide a justification.
detail.ai_failed_banner_detail: Review is proceeding without an AI recommendation. Reason: {{reason}}
detail.ai_failed_accordion_body: The previous AI analysis didn't complete, so no risk score is available. You can re-analyze to try again.
detail.ai_skipped_body: AI analysis was skipped — this datasource has AI analysis disabled.
detail.timeline_ai_skipped_who: skipped — AI disabled for this datasource
detail.execution_failed_body: The query could not be executed. The database returned:
detail.diff_empty: No previous run found to compare against
detail.review_required_subtitle: Approve to forward to execution, reject to send back to submitter.
detail.review_request_changes_required: A comment is required when requesting changes.
detail.review_reject_required: A comment is required when rejecting a query.
detail.cancel_confirm_body: The query will be marked CANCELLED and removed from the review queue. This cannot be undone.
detail.cancel_schedule_confirm_body: The query will be marked CANCELLED and the scheduled execution will not fire.
detail.scheduled_banner_body: AccessFlow will execute this approved query automatically at {{when}}. You can still cancel or run it now.
detail.scheduled_banner_body_pending: If approved, this query will run automatically at {{when}}.
detail.replay_modal_body: Re-run this query's exact SQL against a test datasource. It re-enters the full review workflow — approval is never skipped.
detail.replay_success: Query replayed and submitted for review
detail.replay_no_targets: No other active datasource of the same type is available to replay against.
detail.timeout_body_with_plan: Plan "{{plan}}" requires approval within {{hours}}h. Timed out {{when}}.
detail.timeout_body_without_plan: Approval window of {{hours}}h elapsed without a decision. Timed out {{when}}.

reviews.title: Review queue
reviews.subtitle: Queries awaiting your approval. Newest first.
reviews.empty_description: No queries are waiting on you right now.
reviews.reject_modal_placeholder: Explain why this query is being rejected…

bulk.modal_subtitle_one: One shared comment will be recorded against {{count}} query.
bulk.modal_subtitle_other: One shared comment will be recorded against {{count}} queries.
bulk.comment_placeholder: Comment that will apply to every selected query…
bulk.comment_required: A comment is required for rejection and change requests.
bulk.toast_partial: {{success}} decided · {{failure}} failed — review the highlighted rows.

decide.title: Approve or reject
decide.subtitle: Confirm your identity to commit this decision
decide.step_up_help: Required to confirm a decision from a push notification
decide.own_query: You can't review your own query.
decide.not_pending: This query is no longer awaiting review.

list.title: Datasources
list.subtitle: Connected customer databases proxied through AccessFlow.
list.empty: No datasources yet — add one to get started.

create.title: Add datasource
create.subtitle: Connect a new customer database. AccessFlow will proxy and govern queries against it.
create.settings_step_subtitle: Configure limits and review policies for this datasource. You can change these later from the datasource settings page.
create.label_pool_size_help: Maximum number of concurrent JDBC connections AccessFlow keeps open to this datasource (1–200).
create.label_max_rows_help: Hard cap on rows returned for any single query routed through AccessFlow (1–1,000,000).
create.driver_status_bundled_hint: Driver ships inside AccessFlow. No network call is needed to use this database type.
create.driver_status_downloaded_hint: Driver has been downloaded from Maven Central and cached locally. Ready to use without network access.
create.driver_status_available_hint: Driver is not yet on disk. AccessFlow will download it from Maven Central on first use (typically 1–5 s) and cache it for next time.
create.driver_status_unavailable_hint: Driver cannot be obtained. Offline mode is enabled with an empty cache, or the cache directory is not writable. Check ACCESSFLOW_DRIVERS_OFFLINE and ACCESSFLOW_DRIVER_CACHE.
create.driver_policy_callout_body: AccessFlow bundles the PostgreSQL driver. Other database drivers are downloaded from Maven Central on first use, verified against pinned checksums, and cached locally for reuse.

type_description.postgresql: Open-source relational database. Driver bundled with AccessFlow.
type_description.mysql: Widely used open-source relational database from Oracle.
type_description.oracle: Oracle Database with the official driver.
type_description.mssql: Microsoft SQL Server with the official Microsoft driver.
type_description.mongodb: Document-oriented NoSQL database. Engine installs on demand.
type_description.couchbase: Distributed document database with SQL++ (N1QL). Engine installs on demand.
type_description.redis: In-memory key-value data store. Engine installs on demand.
type_description.cassandra: Wide-column NoSQL database using CQL. Engine installs on demand.
type_description.scylladb: CQL-compatible wide-column database. Engine installs on demand.
type_description.elasticsearch: Distributed search and analytics engine (JSON Query DSL). Engine installs on demand.
type_description.opensearch: Elasticsearch-compatible search engine (JSON Query DSL). Engine installs on demand.
type_description.dynamodb: Amazon serverless key-value and document database (PartiQL). Engine installs on demand.
type_description.neo4j: Graph database using Cypher over Bolt. Engine installs on demand.
type_description.custom: Free-form JDBC connection using an admin-uploaded driver.

create.source_uploaded_help: Admin-uploaded JDBC drivers for your organization. Manage them under Admin → Custom JDBC drivers.
create.no_custom_drivers_hint: No custom drivers uploaded yet — admins can add one to support new database types or driver versions.
create.field_jdbc_url_help: Full JDBC connection string passed verbatim to the uploaded driver.
create.dynamic_mode_notice_body: This database type uses the uploaded JDBC driver. Provide the complete JDBC URL — AccessFlow does not build it for you.
create.field_local_datacenter_help: Cassandra / ScyllaDB load-balancing datacenter (e.g. datacenter1) — required for the driver.
create.field_region_help: DynamoDB region (e.g. us-east-1) — used for request signing.
create.field_dynamodb_endpoint_help: Optional DynamoDB Local / VPC endpoint (e.g. http://localhost:8000). Leave blank for AWS.
create.field_neo4j_bolt_uri_help: Optional full bolt:// / neo4j+s:// URI for Neo4j Aura or clustered routing (e.g. neo4j+s://xxxx.databases.neo4j.io). When set, it overrides host/port.
create.field_api_key_help: Elasticsearch / OpenSearch API key, sent as Authorization: ApiKey. Encrypted at rest.
create.field_text_to_sql_help: Let users draft SQL from natural language (uses the AI configuration).
create.field_ai_config_required: Select an AI configuration or disable AI analysis
create.mysql_public_key_retrieval_body: MySQL 8+ uses caching_sha2_password by default and refuses to authenticate over a plain connection unless the client can fetch the server's RSA public key. AccessFlow adds allowPublicKeyRetrieval=true to the JDBC URL so login completes. The password is sent encrypted with that key, but the rest of the session is unencrypted. Choose REQUIRE or stronger to use TLS for the whole connection.
create.test_resolving_driver_hint: Downloading and verifying the driver. This only happens the first time.
create.test_failure_unknown: The connection could not be established. Check the credentials or network reachability.
create.test_step_subtitle: Run a one-shot SELECT 1 against the new datasource to confirm the driver loads and the credentials work.
create.types_error_body: The /datasources/types endpoint did not respond. Refresh the page or check your backend logs.
create.source_connector_help: Pre-packaged drivers installed from the connector catalog.

settings.delete_confirm_body: {{name}} will be marked inactive. Existing queries are preserved.

masking.title: Masking policies
masking.description: Render sensitive column values with a masking strategy. Users listed under "reveal to" see the unmasked value; everyone else sees the masked output. This extends the static restricted-columns masking.
masking.empty_description: Add a policy to mask a column's value for non-revealed users.
masking.delete_confirm_body: The column will revert to its default rendering for all users.
masking.column_max: Target column must be at most 512 characters
masking.visible_suffix_range: Visible suffix must be between 1 and 256

settings.er_diagram_empty_description: This database has no foreign-key constraints, so there are no relationships to draw.
settings.er_diagram_help: Click a table to highlight its relationships. Click the background to clear.
settings.replica_jdbc_url_help: SELECT queries are routed to this replica; INSERT/UPDATE/DDL always hit the primary. Leave blank to disable replica routing.
settings.replica_password_placeholder: Leave blank to reuse the saved replica password
settings.text_to_sql_help: Let users draft SQL from natural language (uses the AI configuration).
settings.ai_config_required: Pick an AI configuration or disable AI analysis
settings.permissions_subtitle: Granular per-user grants for this datasource. Admins automatically have full access.
settings.grant_user_required: Pick a user to grant access to.
settings.grant_perm_break_glass_help: Additional emergency capability. Lets this user bypass review and execute immediately, with all admins notified and the action prominently audited. It does not stand alone — the user must also hold the read, write, or DDL capability for the query type at submission time.
settings.grant_break_glass_no_expiry_hint: Break-glass is a high-privilege grant — consider setting an expiry.
settings.grant_row_limit_help: Optional. Caps the rows this user can fetch in a single query.
settings.grant_row_limit_min: Row limit must be at least 1.
settings.grant_schemas_help: Leave empty for full schema access. Press Enter after each schema name.
settings.grant_tables_help: Leave empty for full table access. Press Enter after each table name.
settings.grant_columns_help: Values in these columns are masked in query results. The AI reviewer is informed but does not auto-reject.
settings.grant_expires_help: Optional. Access auto-revokes after this date.
settings.grant_expires_future: Expiry must be in the future.
settings.grant_at_least_one_perm: Grant at least one of read, write, or DDL.
settings.grant_already_exists: This user already has a permission row for this datasource.
settings.grant_user_not_in_org: That user is not part of your organization.
settings.activity_empty: No queries have been submitted against this datasource yet.

row_security.title: Row security policies
row_security.description: Filter the rows a user can see (SELECT) or change (UPDATE/DELETE) on a table. Each policy compares a column against a per-user value; users in scope only see authorised rows.
row_security.empty_description: Add a policy to restrict which rows a user can access on a table.
row_security.delete_confirm_body: The table will no longer be row-filtered for affected users.
row_security.table_max: Target table must be at most 512 characters
row_security.column_max: Target column must be at most 512 characters
row_security.value_max: Value must be at most 512 characters
row_security.save_error: Failed to save row security policy
row_security.delete_error: Failed to delete row security policy

classification.title: Data classification
classification.description: Tag tables and columns with data classifications (PII, PCI, PHI, GDPR…). Tagging a column auto-applies a masking policy, raises the AI risk score for queries that touch it, and feeds compliance reporting.
classification.empty_description: Tag a table or column to derive stricter masking and review handling automatically.
classification.delete_confirm_body: The derived masking policy is kept — remove it separately on the Masking tab if no longer needed.
classification.table_max: Table must be at most 256 characters
classification.placeholder_column: column (leave blank to tag the whole table)
classification.column_max: Column must be at most 256 characters
classification.column_hint: Leave blank to tag the whole table (no masking is derived for table-level tags).
classification.apply_masking_hint: Create a masking policy for this column from the classification default (skipped if one already covers it).
classification.apply_masking_table_hint: Masking is only derived for column-level tags.
classification.note_max: Note must be at most 1000 characters
classification.derivation_description: The stricter masking and review posture implied by these tags. Masking is applied automatically; the review posture is a suggestion — apply it on the datasource's review plan.
classification.no_masking_suggestions: No column-level tags to derive masking from.

settings.sample_governed_note: Row-level security and column masking are applied — masked columns never show raw values.
settings.sample_truncated: {{count}} rows (capped by the proxy row limit)
settings.sample_select_prompt: Browse the schema and preview a bounded, governed sample of any table's rows.

organizations.title: Organizations
organizations.subtitle: Manage tenant organizations and their per-org quotas.
organizations.disable_confirm_body: Users in {{name}} will be blocked from signing in and from running queries until it is re-enabled.
organizations.slug_help: URL-safe identifier. Leave blank to derive from the name.

setup_progress.title: Finish setting up AccessFlow
setup_progress.subtitle: Complete these steps to start reviewing queries.

groups.title: User groups
groups.description: Organise reviewers and other users into groups. Groups can be assigned as datasource reviewers and auto-synced from SAML/OAuth IdP claims.
groups.delete_confirm: Delete group "{{name}}"? Memberships and reviewer assignments will be removed.

validation.name_size: Group name must be 1 to 128 characters
validation.description_size: Description must be at most 512 characters

empty.title: No groups yet
empty.description: Create groups to bundle reviewers and assign them to datasources.

members_empty.title: No members yet
members_empty.description: Add users to this group to delegate reviewer access.

users.title: Users
users.password_help: 8–128 characters. The user can change it after first login.
users.deactivate_confirm_body: {{email}} will be deactivated and signed out of all sessions.
users.invite_revoke_confirm_body: The invitation link for {{email}} will stop working.
users.attributes_hint: Key/value attributes resolvable in row-security predicates as :user.<key>.

audit.title: Audit log
audit.subtitle: Append-only, tamper-evident log of every action in the system.
audit.empty: No audit events match the current filter.
audit.export_failed: Failed to export audit log. Please try again.
audit.export_truncated: Export truncated at 50,000 rows. Apply tighter filters to capture the full result set.

ai_analyses.title: AI analyses
ai_analyses.subtitle: Trends across every query the AI has reviewed: risk over time, hot issue categories, and most active submitters.
ai_analyses.empty_title: No AI analyses in this window
ai_analyses.empty_description: Pick a wider date range, or submit queries against a datasource with AI analysis enabled.

ai_configs.title: AI configurations
ai_configs.subtitle: Configure as many AI providers as you need. Each datasource binds to one.
ai_configs.empty_body: Add an AI configuration to enable risk analysis on a datasource.
ai_configs.delete_confirm_body: This will remove the configuration named {{name}}.
ai_configs.system_prompt_help: Leave blank to use the built-in default. Available placeholders: {{sql}}, {{schema_context}}, {{db_type}}, {{language}}.
ai_configs.system_prompt_sql_required: A custom prompt must contain the {{sql}} placeholder
ai_configs.system_prompt_load_error: Failed to load the default prompt
ai_configs.api_key_required: API key is required for this provider
ai_configs.endpoint_required: API endpoint is required for a custom provider

wizard.title: New AI configuration
wizard.subtitle: Pick a provider, fill in the connection details, and run a test prompt.

ai_configs.langfuse_prompt_help: Optional. When set and Langfuse prompt management is enabled, the analyzer fetches this prompt from Langfuse instead of the system prompt above.

rag.section_help: Retrieve organization-specific knowledge and inject it into AI analysis and SQL generation.
rag.pgvector_unavailable_warning: In-app pgvector storage is unavailable on this deployment
rag.pgvector_unavailable_help: Select Qdrant or install the PostgreSQL vector extension to use in-app storage.
rag.store_type_required: A vector store is required when RAG is enabled
rag.endpoint_required: An endpoint is required for an external vector store
rag.collection_required: A collection name is required for an external vector store
rag.threshold_range: Similarity threshold must be between 0 and 1
rag.documents_help: Documents are chunked, embedded and stored for retrieval. Add them after saving the configuration.
rag.documents_save_first: Save the configuration before adding knowledge documents.

orchestration.section_help: Run several models in parallel and combine their verdicts by voting. The primary model above votes alongside the members below.
orchestration.voting_weight_help: Relative weight of the primary model in the weighted vote.
orchestration.member_endpoint_required: An endpoint is required for an OpenAI-compatible model

guardrails.section_help: Block queries whose text matches a configured pattern before any model is called. Each pattern is a case-insensitive regular expression.

notifications.title: Notification channels
notifications.subtitle: Where AccessFlow sends review events, approvals, and security alerts.
notifications.empty: No notification channels yet — add one to start receiving alerts.
notifications.delete_confirm_body: This will remove the channel named {{name}}.

system_smtp.title: System SMTP
system_smtp.subtitle: Used for user-invitation emails and as a fallback when no email channel is configured.
system_smtp.delete_confirm: Remove the system SMTP configuration? User invitations and the email fallback will no longer work until reconfigured.
system_smtp.password_help: Leave blank to keep the existing password.
system_smtp.test_success: Test email accepted by the SMTP server
system_smtp.not_configured: System SMTP is not configured. Configure it to enable user invitations and the email fallback.

oauth2.title: OAuth providers
oauth2.subtitle: Configure one or more OAuth 2.0 / OIDC providers so users can sign in with their existing accounts.

setup_intro.google: Open the <console>Google Cloud Console → APIs & Services → Credentials</console>, create an OAuth 2.0 Client ID of type "Web application", and paste the redirect URI below into "Authorized redirect URIs".
setup_intro.github: Open <console>GitHub → Settings → Developer settings → OAuth Apps</console>, click "New OAuth App", and paste the redirect URI below into "Authorization callback URL".
setup_intro.microsoft: Open the <console>Microsoft Entra admin center → App registrations</console>, register a new application, and under Authentication → Redirect URIs add a Web platform entry with the URI below. Capture the tenant ID from the app's overview page.
setup_intro.gitlab: Open <console>GitLab → User Settings → Applications</console> (or Admin Area → Applications for an org-wide app), and paste the redirect URI below into "Redirect URI".
setup_intro.oidc: Register a new OAuth 2.0 / OIDC client in your identity provider (Keycloak, Auth0, Okta, Authentik, Zitadel, etc.). Use the redirect URI below as the allowed callback. Then paste your provider's endpoint URLs into the fields below — most IdPs publish them at /.well-known/openid-configuration.
setup_intro.github_enterprise: On your self-hosted GitHub Enterprise Server, open your profile → Settings → Developer settings → OAuth Apps, register a new app, and paste the redirect URI below into "Authorization callback URL". Then enter your instance's base URL below.
setup_intro.gitlab_enterprise: On your self-managed GitLab instance, open User Settings → Applications (or Admin Area → Applications for an instance-wide app), register a new application with the redirect URI below, and request the scopes openid, email, profile. Then enter your instance's base URL below.

oauth2.callback_url_label: Redirect URI to register with the provider
oauth2.callback_url_help: Copy this exact URL into the provider's developer console. AccessFlow accepts the callback only at this path.
oauth2.label_client_secret_help: Leave the masked value untouched to keep the existing secret. Clear it to remove the secret.
oauth2.label_scopes_help: Space-separated. Leave empty to use the provider defaults.
oauth2.label_tenant_id_help: Use "common" for any Microsoft account, "organizations" for any work/school account, or a specific tenant GUID.

allowed_orgs_help.google: Google enforces this via email domain — use the field below. This list is ignored for Google.
allowed_orgs_help.github: GitHub organization logins (case-sensitive). Requires the read:org scope. Empty = any GitHub user may sign in.
allowed_orgs_help.microsoft: Microsoft Entra AAD group object IDs. The app must be configured to emit the "groups" claim. Empty = any user from the tenant may sign in.
allowed_orgs_help.gitlab: GitLab full group paths from the "groups" OIDC claim. Empty = any GitLab user may sign in.
allowed_orgs_help.oidc: Group identifiers from the claim configured below as "Groups attribute". Empty = any user authenticated by the IdP may sign in (or restrict by email domain).
allowed_orgs_help.github_enterprise: GitHub Enterprise organization logins (case-sensitive). Requires the read:org scope. Empty = any GitHub Enterprise user may sign in.
allowed_orgs_help.gitlab_enterprise: Full group paths from the OIDC groups claim on your self-managed GitLab (e.g. acme/team). Empty = any GitLab user may sign in.

allowed_orgs_placeholder.google: Use the email domains field below

oauth2.allowed_domains_help: Comma- or space-separated. Matched case-insensitively against the email domain. Empty = any email domain accepted.
oauth2.github_read_org_scope_warning_title: GitHub will not return private org membership
oauth2.github_read_org_scope_warning: An allowed-organizations list is set but the scopes override does not include read:org. Saving while active is enabled will be rejected by the server. Add read:org to the scopes override.

client_id_tooltip.google: The OAuth 2.0 Client ID created in the Google Cloud Console. Looks like 1234567890-abc.apps.googleusercontent.com.
client_id_tooltip.github: The GitHub OAuth App's Client ID (visible on the app's settings page once created).
client_id_tooltip.microsoft: The Application (client) ID shown on the app's Overview page in the Entra admin center.
client_id_tooltip.gitlab: The Application ID shown on the application's page after creation in GitLab.
client_id_tooltip.oidc: The OIDC client identifier registered with your identity provider.
client_id_tooltip.github_enterprise: The Client ID from the OAuth App you registered on your GitHub Enterprise Server.
client_id_tooltip.gitlab_enterprise: The Application ID from the OAuth application you registered on your self-managed GitLab.

client_secret_tooltip.google: Click "Create credentials" in Google Cloud and download the secret — it is only shown once.
client_secret_tooltip.github: Generate a new client secret on the GitHub OAuth App page. Copy it immediately; GitHub does not show it again.
client_secret_tooltip.microsoft: Under Certificates & secrets, create a new Client Secret. Copy the "Value" (not the Secret ID).
client_secret_tooltip.gitlab: Shown once on the application page right after creation under "Secret".
client_secret_tooltip.oidc: The OIDC client secret issued by your identity provider when the client was registered.
client_secret_tooltip.github_enterprise: The Client Secret generated for your GitHub Enterprise Server OAuth App. Copy it immediately — it is shown only once.
client_secret_tooltip.gitlab_enterprise: Shown once on your self-managed GitLab application page after creation.

oauth2.label_base_url_help: Origin of your self-hosted instance, e.g. https://github.acme.corp — no trailing path, no query string.

base_url_tooltip.github_enterprise: Base URL of your GitHub Enterprise Server. AccessFlow appends /login/oauth/authorize, /login/oauth/access_token, and /api/v3/* automatically.
base_url_tooltip.gitlab_enterprise: Base URL of your self-managed GitLab instance. AccessFlow appends /oauth/authorize, /oauth/token, /oauth/userinfo, and /oauth/discovery/keys automatically.

oauth2.label_display_name_help: Shown on the login page on the OAuth button ("Continue with …").
oauth2.label_groups_attribute_help: Required if Allowed organizations is set. Name of the claim that lists the user's groups.
oauth2.help_oidc_attribute_defaults: Attribute claim names are optional — leave blank to use the standard OIDC defaults (sub, email, email_verified, name).
oauth2.scopes_tooltip: Space- or comma-separated. Leave empty to use the provider defaults ({{defaults}}).
oauth2.tenant_id_tooltip: Microsoft Entra tenant ID, GUID, or a well-known alias: "common" (any Microsoft account), "organizations" (any work or school account), or "consumers" (personal accounts only).
oauth2.delete_confirm: Remove this provider configuration? Users currently signed in stay signed in.

saml.title: SAML / SSO
saml.subtitle: Configure your identity provider for single sign-on.

languages.title: Languages
languages.subtitle: Pick which languages users can choose, the default for new accounts, and the language the AI analyzer responds in.
languages.section_user_languages_help: Users can pick their UI language from this list.
languages.section_ai_language_help: AI analysis summaries and suggestions are produced in this language for every query in the org. Risk levels stay in English.

data_classifications.title: Data classifications
data_classifications.subtitle: Every data-classification tag across datasources — the evidence base for compliance reporting.
data_classifications.empty_description: Tag tables and columns from a datasource's Classification tab to populate this report.

review_plans.title: Review plans
review_plans.subtitle: Configurations that drive AI and human review for queries against datasources.
review_plans.empty: No review plans yet — add one to start governing query review.
review_plans.delete_confirm_body: {{name}} will be removed. This is permanent.
review_plans.validation_min_approvers: Add at least one approver when human approval is required.
review_plans.validation_user_or_role: Each approver row needs a user or a role.

STRICT_WRITES_2_APPROVALS.name: Strict — writes need 2 approvals
STRICT_WRITES_2_APPROVALS.description: AI required, two reviewers must approve every write.

LENIENT_READS_AUTO_APPROVED.name: Lenient — reads auto-approved
LENIENT_READS_AUTO_APPROVED.description: Low/medium-risk reads are auto-approved; writes need one reviewer.

AI_ONLY_NO_HUMAN.name: AI-only — no human approval
AI_ONLY_NO_HUMAN.description: AI analyzes every query; no human reviewer is required.

STANDARD_AI_PLUS_ONE_REVIEWER.name: Standard — AI + 1 reviewer
STANDARD_AI_PLUS_ONE_REVIEWER.description: AI plus a single reviewer; low/medium-risk reads auto-approved.

datasource_health.title: Datasource health
datasource_health.subtitle: Connection-pool utilisation, query volume, latency, and errors per datasource (last 24h).
datasource_health.empty_description: Add a datasource to start seeing connection-pool and query-volume signals here.
datasource_health.error_description: The health snapshot failed to load. Try again.
datasource_health.pool_not_initialized: Pool not initialised — no queries since startup.

slack.title: Slack app
slack.subtitle: Connect a Slack app so reviewers can approve or reject queries from Slack.
slack.status_configured: A Slack app is configured for this organization.
slack.status_unconfigured: No Slack app is configured yet.
slack.load_error: Failed to load the Slack app configuration

routing_policies.title: Routing policies
routing_policies.subtitle: Attribute-based rules that auto-decide query routing before reviewer fan-out.
routing_policies.empty: No routing policies yet. Queries fall through to the datasource review plan.
routing_policies.delete_confirm_body: Delete "{{name}}"? Queries will no longer be routed by this rule.
routing_policies.condition_advanced_warning: This policy uses a nested condition the guided builder can't edit; saving here will replace it.

langfuse.title: Langfuse
langfuse.subtitle: Connect Langfuse for LLM tracing and prompt management.
langfuse.host_help: Langfuse base URL. Leave blank to use the default (https://cloud.langfuse.com).
langfuse.secret_key_help: Project secret key (sk-lf-…). Stored encrypted; never shown again.
langfuse.tracing_help: Emit a trace to Langfuse for every AI analysis.
langfuse.prompt_management_help: Fetch analyzer prompts from Langfuse by name on AI configurations.

events.REVIEW_TIMEOUT: Your query on {{datasource}} timed out awaiting review

display_name.title: Profile
display_name.subtitle: How your name appears across AccessFlow.
display_name.label: Display name

password.title: Password
password.subtitle: Choose a strong password you don't use elsewhere.
password.saved: Password updated. You'll be signed out of other sessions.
password.saml_disabled: Password is managed by your SAML identity provider.
password.oauth2_disabled: Password is managed by your OAuth provider. Update it in that provider's account settings.

totp.title: Two-factor authentication
totp.subtitle_disabled: Add a second step to sign-in using an authenticator app.
totp.subtitle_enabled: Two-factor authentication is enabled on this account.
totp.saml_disabled: Two-factor authentication is managed by your SAML identity provider.
totp.oauth2_disabled: Two-factor authentication is managed by your OAuth provider. Enable it in that provider's account settings.
totp.step_scan_help: Open Google Authenticator, 1Password, or any TOTP app and scan this code.
totp.step_verify_help: Confirm setup by entering the code your authenticator shows.
totp.backup_codes_help: Store these recovery codes somewhere safe. Each can be used once if you lose access to your authenticator.

api_keys.title: API keys
api_keys.description: Create personal API keys to authenticate the AccessFlow MCP server and other programmatic clients. Keys inherit your permissions and can be revoked at any time.
api_keys.name_size: Name must be between 1 and 100 characters.
api_keys.copy_once_warning: This is the only time the key is shown. Copy it now and store it somewhere safe — you won't be able to retrieve it again.
api_keys.empty: You haven't created any API keys yet.
api_keys.revoke_confirm: Revoke API key "{{name}}"? Clients using it will lose access immediately.

slack.title: Slack account
slack.description: Link your Slack account to approve or reject queries from Slack.
slack.code_ready: Run this command in Slack to finish linking:
slack.code_instructions: The code expires shortly. Generate a new one if it stops working.

totp_login.title: Two-factor authentication
totp_login.subtitle: Enter the 6-digit code from your authenticator app.

list.title: Custom JDBC drivers
list.subtitle: Upload vendor-specific or in-house JDBC driver JARs for your organization. Datasources can then bind to these drivers instead of the bundled set.
list.empty_description: Upload a JDBC driver JAR to support forks, vendor builds, or database types beyond the bundled five.
list.delete_confirm_body: {{vendor}} ({{filename}}) will be removed. Any datasource that still references it must be deleted first.

upload.title: Upload JDBC driver
upload.subtitle: Select the driver JAR and enter the SHA-256 you computed locally. AccessFlow verifies the checksum before storing and loads the driver class to confirm it implements java.sql.Driver.
upload.target_db_type_help: Pick the matching bundled type to override its driver, or 'Custom / Dynamic' for a free-form JDBC connection.
upload.driver_class_help: Fully-qualified Java class name of the JDBC driver inside the JAR.
upload.sha256_help: Run `shasum -a 256 driver.jar` locally and paste the hex digest. AccessFlow rejects the upload if the bytes don't match.
upload.missing_jar: Select a JAR file before uploading.

validation.vendor_max: Vendor name must be at most 100 characters.
validation.driver_class_max: Driver class must be at most 255 characters.
validation.driver_class_pattern: Enter a fully-qualified Java class name (e.g. com.vendor.Driver).
